title: Classify-Execute
description: Classify an item into a known class, then run the specialist execute path for that class.
openingSummary: Classify-execute is a two-stage technique: label or route an item into a known class, then run the specialist path for that class.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Classify-execute is a two-stage technique: a classify step assigns an item to a known class or route, then an execute step runs the specialist path for that class.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Separating triage from specialized doing lets each execute path use a tighter prompt, model, or tool set instead of one mixed-intent pass for every class.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Intake an item, classify it into a bounded set of classes, hand it to the matching execute path, and complete or escalate when no class fits.

comparedToNearbyTechniques.title: Compared To Nearby Techniques
comparedToNearbyTechniques.body: Classify-execute labels an item then runs its specialist path. Nearby techniques such as planner-executor, writer-reviewer, and workqueue-executor organize different factory jobs and are not the same pattern.
